source.climateVerificationNote: No climate source URL is linked for this location.

desc: Arugam Bay is Sri Lanka's flagship east-coast surf town with a pronounced seasonal rhythm: dry, cleaner-swell months in mid-year and wetter, quieter off-season periods. It suits surfers, slow beach stays, and wildlife add-ons when you plan around monsoon timing.

todo.name: Main Point surf sessions
todo.desc: Target dawn and late-afternoon windows when wind and crowds are better.

todo.name: Whiskey Point or Peanut Farm
todo.desc: Rotate breaks by swell direction and your board level.

todo.name: Lagoon safari at Pottuvil
todo.desc: Early-morning boat rides for birds, mangroves, and crocodile spotting.

todo.name: Kudumbigala monastery trip
todo.desc: Combine a short hike with panoramic coastal views.

todo.name: Kumana National Park day trip
todo.desc: Plan a dry-season safari for birds, elephants, and quieter tracks.

todo.name: Beachfront café evening
todo.desc: Wind down with seafood grills and low-key surf-town nightlife.

wifi.note: Adequate for basic remote tasks in most guesthouses, but rain, power cuts, and local infrastructure can reduce reliability.

prac.fltNote: Usual route is Gatwick to Colombo (CMB) then long overland transfer to Arugam Bay (often 7–9+ hours). Return fares are often ~£500–£780 off-peak, ~£780–£1,020 in shoulder demand, and ~£1,020+ around major holiday peaks.
prac.lang: Tamil, Sinhala, English common in surf tourism
prac.visa: Most visitors require an ETA/eVisa before arrival; confirm current Sri Lanka entry rules before travel.
alerts: Long transfer times from Colombo are the norm; avoid tight same-day flight connections.
Off-season rain and swell shifts can limit surf consistency at some breaks.
Power interruptions still occur in some areas — keep offline backups for navigation/work.

sweet: May to September is generally best for east-coast surf conditions and drier beach weather.
